Key Coverages of Standard Cover There are four key sections: Section One: Building cover* Depending on your choice of coverage, provides cover for loss or damage to Your Building caused by an Accident or a Named Peril during the Policy Period Section Two: Contents cover** Provides cover for loss or damage to Contents caused by an Accident whilst contained in the Building during the Policy Period Section Three: Worldwide Family Liability (Excluding USA/Canada) Covers against claim from Third Party for property damage or bodily injury caused to them which You and Your Family is/are legally liable. Section Four: Personal Accident Covers You and Your Family against death or permanent disability as a result of an accidental injury. Includes linked panel or specialist resources.

Options Choose among our four offerings: Plan A Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $50,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $2,000 Plan B Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $100,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $4,000 Plan C Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $200,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $6,000 Plan D Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $300,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $8,000 Important Notes Here is are some key points to take note: All amounts shown are in Singapore Dollars Benefits under this insurance will be payable only upon death, permanent disablement or injury due to an accident. All those who are insured must be a Singapore citizen, Singapore Permanent Resident or foreigner who is holding a valid employment pass, work permit, dependent pass, student pass or social visit pass and residing in Singapore. plans.plan_description: Secure your journey, safeguard your vehicle with our comprehensive motor insurance From as low as S$500/Year
plans.plan_overview: Secure your journey, safeguard your vehicle with our comprehensive motor insurance From as low as S$500/Year As a driver, driving a vehicle on the road carries different risks, whether it is damage to your own car or getting a claim from a third-party in an accident. If you have just purchased a new car or bike, you would want to get your vehicle insured as soon as possible. Motor insurance is one of the key essentials, yet often overlooked aspects of owning a motor vehicle in Singapore. There are several types of bike and car insurance plans as well as insurers to select from in Singapore. However, knowing which one suits your needs best is crucial. Most motor vehicle owners typically go for the cheapest car insurance. However, rather than prioritising just cost, being aware of what the policy covers and the variety of coverage available are the ideal ways to purchase the best motor insurance . If you are looking for affordable motor vehicle insurance, India International Insurance is able to provide you with the right solution. With our comprehensive coverage, you are assured of protection and coverage of your motor vehicle, the other party's motor vehicle, and, most importantly, yourself while driving or riding on the road. Our policy also provides you with added coverage over loss from natural disasters and theft.

What is a Whole Life insurance?
A Whole Life insurance is a way to protect your loved ones financially when you pass away.  The payouts provide your beneficiaries the financial resource at their discretion. You can choose from different types of life insurance plans in Singapore, depending on your needs and goals.
Why is a Whole Life insurance important
If you are worrying whether your loved ones are financially secured when you are not around, you may consider a Whole Life insurance plan that can protect them from financial hardships and help them achieve their dreams. A life insurance policy is a way of creating your lasting legacy of your values and vision.
How is Whole Life insurance different from Term Insurance?
Whole Life insurance provides lifetime protection, while Term insurance offers coverage for a predetermined period. Additionally, Whole Life insurance usually includes a cash value component that grows over time, creating a savings component alongside the death benefit protection. Term insurance only provides insurance coverage without any savings element.
What are the key features of a Whole Life insurance plan?
Whole Life insurance generally provides lifetime coverage and a guaranteed cash value component. Premium payments are generally also structured to remain level and locked in a policy inception. The death benefit component provides a guaranteed payout of sum insured and any bonuses to beneficiaries.
What are the benefits of Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance provides lifelong financial protection for your beneficiaries, offering coverage that remains in force regardless of changes in age or health status. The cash value accumulation creates disciplined wealth building through tax-deferred growth, providing accessible funds (via partial surrenders) when needed such as an emergency fund, or fund major expenses like education. The death benefit also facilitates efficient wealth transfer while helping to preserve family wealth across generations.
Who should consider buying Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance is suitable for individuals who want lifelong protection and long-term saving.  It is also ideal for High-Net-Worth individuals who seek additional wealth accumulation.
What should I consider before buying Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance is designed to provide lifelong coverage, with the potential to accumulate cash value over time. When considering this type of policy, it is important to think about your long‑term objectives, such as providing financial protection, meeting future obligations, or planning a legacy for beneficiaries. You may also wish to consider the level of coverage required, the premium payment commitment, and whether the policy’s features and access to cash value align with your long‑term financial plans.

What is an Indexed Universal life insurance (IUL) ?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance is a life insurance that offers lifetime coverage and the indexed accounts to grow your cash value. The crediting rate to an indexed account is linked to the performance of an underlying index, such as the S&P 500, with a minimum guaranteed floor. An Indexed Universal Life (IUL) policy also allows you to adjust your premium payments and death benefits within certain limits, helping you protect and grow your wealth based on your protection needs and financial goals.
Why is an Indexed Universal Life (IUL)  insurance important?
Life insurance can be used in your wealth planning for its insurance coverage and certain underlying guaranteed components. An Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers life coverage and allows you to participate in the potential upside of an index while having the built-in downside protection.
How is Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance different from Whole Life insurance?
Both Indexed Universal Life insurance and Whole Life insurance are permanent life insurance policies that provide lifelong coverage, but they work differently.  Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers more flexibility, allowing you to adjust premiums and death benefits, with cash value growth tied to market index performance (e.g. S&P 500). Whole Life insurance offers predictable, guaranteed cash value growth at rates predetermined by the insurance company, with fixed premiums and guaranteed benefits.
What are the key features of an Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance plan?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers flexibility in premium payments and death benefits, allowing adjustments within policy limits as your financial circumstances change. The cash value can grow through fixed interest rate accounts for stable returns or indexed accounts that track market performance for potential higher growth. IUL policies include built-in protection mechanisms: a "floor" (minimum interest rate, often 0%) ensures your returns are not below the floor rate during market downturns, while a "cap" limits maximum gains during strong market performance.
What are the benefits of Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers potential for higher cash value growth compared to traditional whole life insurance when markets perform well, flexibility to adjust premiums and death benefits based on changing financial circumstances, and the ability to access cash value through loans or partial surrenders. Additionally, the policy provides downside protection against market losses while allowing participation in market upside.
Who should consider buying Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance?
IUL may be more suitable for individuals who want additional wealth growth opportunities, business owners seeking estate planning solutions, or individuals seeking retirement planning strategies with lifelong protection.
What should I consider before buying Indexed Universal Life insurance?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers lifetime coverage with policy value growth linked to the performance of a market index, subject to policy terms such as caps and charges. Before purchasing, it is important to understand how premiums are allocated, how charges and policy values work, and how index-linked crediting operates. You may also wish to consider whether this type of policy aligns with both your protection needs and long-term accumulation objectives, as policy value growth linked to an index can vary over time.

Choose among our four offerings:

















Plan A

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $50,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $2,000

Plan B

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $100,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $4,000

Plan C

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $200,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $6,000

Plan D

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $300,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $8,000

Important Notes
Here is are some key points to take note:

















All amounts shown are in Singapore Dollars
Benefits under this insurance will be payable only upon death, permanent disablement or injury due to an accident.
All those who are insured must be a Singapore citizen, Singapore Permanent Resident or foreigner who is holding a valid employment pass, work permit, dependent pass, student pass or social visit pass and residing in Singapore.
You or your spouse must be at least 18 years old and below the age of 70 at the commencement of this insurance. If enrolled under this insurance before 65 years old and there is no lapse in cover, this insurance is renewable until the age of 85. However, once you or your spouse is 75 years old and above, only  Plan A, B or C can be selected.
Your dependent child(ren) must be at least 1 month old at the commencement of this insurance and you can continue to cover them under this insurance until they are 21 or 25 (if the child is studying full-time in a tertiary institution).
If you or your spouse is a homemaker, full-time student, retiree or unemployed, only Plan A, B or C can be selected
If you, your spouse or your dependent child(ren) has/have more than one policy with us covering against Terrorism, the maximum amount payable for Acts of Terrorism for all the policies shall not exceed $500,000 per person.
When more than one person is insured under the policy, the total maximum amount payable under the policy is further subjected to a conveyance/event limit.
Premium rates are non-guaranteed and may be reviewed from time to time

You or we may cancel the policy by giving each other prior notice in writing. Please refer to the policy wordings for the cancellation conditions and applicable charges.
If you or your spouse wish(es) to nominate a beneficiary, please contact your intermediary for the relevant forms.

How much is my premium?
Your premium may vary, depending on:

















1
The type of plan selected



 





2
Your nature of work






3
Our underwriting requirements

Simply submit your claim form and any supporting documents to Broadspire at travel-uoi@broadspire.asia, or reach them at +65 6632 8639 within 31 days of your policy’s expiry.

Single Trip: If you cancel before your trip, you will get a refund minus S$25 (per insured person). No refund if the trip has already started. Annual Multi-Trip: If you cancel within 30 days before your policy starts, we’ll refund based on short-term rates, as long as no claims have been made.

You must be a Singapore Citizen, Permanent Resident, or hold a valid Employment Pass, Work Permit, Dependent Pass, or Long-Term Visit Pass in Singapore. Your trip must start and end in Singapore, and it cannot be for medical treatment purposes.

02/03/2026: Due to the escalating security situation in the Middle East, following strikes by the United States and Israel on Iran commencing on Saturday, 28 February 2026, under the terms of our policy, this situation falls under the General Exclusions relating to war and war-like operations, which apply to all sections of the policy. This means the policy does not cover any claim that arises directly or indirectly from war, hostilities, retaliatory strikes, or military actions.

As Singapore's #1 home insurer for customer experience¹, we provide comprehensive protection for your building, home contents, renovations as well as reimbursement for home assistance services, plus many other benefits. FWD Home insurance is also recommended as a complementary product for your Fire insurance (find out more here ).

We provide coverage if your pet dog or cat is stolen, passes away or is injured due to an insured event that happens at your home.

If you’re a landlord and your tenant defaults on rent, we cover up to S$3,000 per month.

plans.plan_overview:
With higher medical coverage for your helper, you'll be financially protected for medical expenses that may arise due to her illness or injury. In line with the Ministry of Manpower's (MOM) requirements, our hospitalisation and surgical expenses limits have increased to S$60,000 for all plans.

In line with MOM’s requirements, our hospitalisation and surgical expenses limits have increased to S$60,000 for all plans. You also have the option to increase the limit up to S$120,000.

We’re the only insurer who does not make you pay if your Security Bond gets forfeited by MOM.

plans.plan_description: Health Protection Protect you and your family against the financial burden of unforeseen injury and illness Our medical, personal accident and critical illness health insurance plans can help manage potential medical costs, so that you can focus on living your best life.
plans.plan_name: Protect you and your family against the financial burden of unforeseen injury and illness
plans.plan_overview:
Contact Us

Things to consider Protect yourself from unforeseen medical expenses with our health insurance plans so that you can focus on recovery. Hospitalisation benefits With rising healthcare costs, our suite of health insurance plans have you covered from diagnosis to recovery. Complement your medical coverage with critical illness protection While hospitalisation insurance reimburses your hospitalisation medical bills and certain outpatient expenses, critical illness insurance offers financial support, usually in a form of a lump sum payout, during your recovery. Available supplementary benefits Enhance your existing health insurance plans to enjoy more holistic coverage and peace of mind.

Finding the right health insurance plan Medical & Accident Critical Illness Be protected against unforeseen medical expenses, rising healthcare costs or unexpected injuries with our suite of tailored products and supplementary plans. PRU Shield and PRU Extra Comprehensive health insurance coverage to suit your budget and needs. PRU Personal Accident Personal accident insurance to cover minor mishaps or unexpected injuries even when you’re overseas. All Medical & Accident Plans Prepare for…

plans.plan_description: Life Protection Provide financial security for you and your family Our life protection insurance plans help keep your family financially secure in the event of the unexpected, such as disability or death.
plans.plan_name: Provide financial security for you and your family
plans.plan_overview:
Contact Us

Things to consider Find the most suitable life protection insurance plan so that you get ultimate peace of mind to achieve milestones. Meet your life insurance needs Get coverage for you and your family needs financially to ensure expenses and quality of life can be maintained should you become unable to work or meet with a premature death. Customisable plans to suit your changing needs Our life insurance protection plans provide flexibility for you to customise according to your changing needs as you progress through different life stages. Get protected at any life stage This includes protection for mum and baby during your journey to motherhood and being financially protected to overcome complications with our maternity insurance plans.

Finding the right life protection plan Whole & Term Life Maternity Mortgage Get comprehensive essential coverage no matter the life stage you’re at. Key Coverages of Standard Cover There are four key sections: Section One: Building cover* Depending on your choice of coverage, provides cover for loss or damage to Your Building caused by an Accident or a Named Peril during the Policy Period Section Two: Contents cover** Provides cover for loss or damage to Contents caused by an Accident whilst contained in the Building during the Policy Period Section Three: Worldwide Family Liability (Excluding USA/Canada) Covers against claim from Third Party for property damage or bodily injury caused to them which You and Your Family is/are legally liable. Section Four: Personal Accident Covers You and Your Family against death or permanent disability as a result of an accidental injury. Includes linked panel or specialist resources.

Options Choose among our four offerings: Plan A Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $50,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $2,000 Plan B Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $100,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $4,000 Plan C Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $200,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $6,000 Plan D Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $300,000 Medical expenses for accidents: $8,000 Important Notes Here is are some key points to take note: All amounts shown are in Singapore Dollars Benefits under this insurance will be payable only upon death, permanent disablement or injury due to an accident. All those who are insured must be a Singapore citizen, Singapore Permanent Resident or foreigner who is holding a valid employment pass, work permit, dependent pass, student pass or social visit pass and residing in Singapore. plans.plan_description: Secure your journey, safeguard your vehicle with our comprehensive motor insurance From as low as S$500/Year
plans.plan_overview: Secure your journey, safeguard your vehicle with our comprehensive motor insurance From as low as S$500/Year As a driver, driving a vehicle on the road carries different risks, whether it is damage to your own car or getting a claim from a third-party in an accident. If you have just purchased a new car or bike, you would want to get your vehicle insured as soon as possible. Motor insurance is one of the key essentials, yet often overlooked aspects of owning a motor vehicle in Singapore. There are several types of bike and car insurance plans as well as insurers to select from in Singapore. However, knowing which one suits your needs best is crucial. Most motor vehicle owners typically go for the cheapest car insurance. However, rather than prioritising just cost, being aware of what the policy covers and the variety of coverage available are the ideal ways to purchase the best motor insurance . If you are looking for affordable motor vehicle insurance, India International Insurance is able to provide you with the right solution. With our comprehensive coverage, you are assured of protection and coverage of your motor vehicle, the other party's motor vehicle, and, most importantly, yourself while driving or riding on the road. Our policy also provides you with added coverage over loss from natural disasters and theft.

What is a Whole Life insurance?
A Whole Life insurance is a way to protect your loved ones financially when you pass away.  The payouts provide your beneficiaries the financial resource at their discretion. You can choose from different types of life insurance plans in Singapore, depending on your needs and goals.
Why is a Whole Life insurance important
If you are worrying whether your loved ones are financially secured when you are not around, you may consider a Whole Life insurance plan that can protect them from financial hardships and help them achieve their dreams. A life insurance policy is a way of creating your lasting legacy of your values and vision.
How is Whole Life insurance different from Term Insurance?
Whole Life insurance provides lifetime protection, while Term insurance offers coverage for a predetermined period. Additionally, Whole Life insurance usually includes a cash value component that grows over time, creating a savings component alongside the death benefit protection. Term insurance only provides insurance coverage without any savings element.
What are the key features of a Whole Life insurance plan?
Whole Life insurance generally provides lifetime coverage and a guaranteed cash value component. Premium payments are generally also structured to remain level and locked in a policy inception. The death benefit component provides a guaranteed payout of sum insured and any bonuses to beneficiaries.
What are the benefits of Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance provides lifelong financial protection for your beneficiaries, offering coverage that remains in force regardless of changes in age or health status. The cash value accumulation creates disciplined wealth building through tax-deferred growth, providing accessible funds (via partial surrenders) when needed such as an emergency fund, or fund major expenses like education. The death benefit also facilitates efficient wealth transfer while helping to preserve family wealth across generations.
Who should consider buying Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance is suitable for individuals who want lifelong protection and long-term saving.  It is also ideal for High-Net-Worth individuals who seek additional wealth accumulation.
What should I consider before buying Whole Life insurance?
Whole Life insurance is designed to provide lifelong coverage, with the potential to accumulate cash value over time. When considering this type of policy, it is important to think about your long‑term objectives, such as providing financial protection, meeting future obligations, or planning a legacy for beneficiaries. You may also wish to consider the level of coverage required, the premium payment commitment, and whether the policy’s features and access to cash value align with your long‑term financial plans.

What is an Indexed Universal life insurance (IUL) ?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance is a life insurance that offers lifetime coverage and the indexed accounts to grow your cash value. The crediting rate to an indexed account is linked to the performance of an underlying index, such as the S&P 500, with a minimum guaranteed floor. An Indexed Universal Life (IUL) policy also allows you to adjust your premium payments and death benefits within certain limits, helping you protect and grow your wealth based on your protection needs and financial goals.
Why is an Indexed Universal Life (IUL)  insurance important?
Life insurance can be used in your wealth planning for its insurance coverage and certain underlying guaranteed components. An Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers life coverage and allows you to participate in the potential upside of an index while having the built-in downside protection.
How is Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance different from Whole Life insurance?
Both Indexed Universal Life insurance and Whole Life insurance are permanent life insurance policies that provide lifelong coverage, but they work differently.  Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers more flexibility, allowing you to adjust premiums and death benefits, with cash value growth tied to market index performance (e.g. S&P 500). Whole Life insurance offers predictable, guaranteed cash value growth at rates predetermined by the insurance company, with fixed premiums and guaranteed benefits.
What are the key features of an Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance plan?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers flexibility in premium payments and death benefits, allowing adjustments within policy limits as your financial circumstances change. The cash value can grow through fixed interest rate accounts for stable returns or indexed accounts that track market performance for potential higher growth. IUL policies include built-in protection mechanisms: a "floor" (minimum interest rate, often 0%) ensures your returns are not below the floor rate during market downturns, while a "cap" limits maximum gains during strong market performance.
What are the benefits of Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers potential for higher cash value growth compared to traditional whole life insurance when markets perform well, flexibility to adjust premiums and death benefits based on changing financial circumstances, and the ability to access cash value through loans or partial surrenders. Additionally, the policy provides downside protection against market losses while allowing participation in market upside.
Who should consider buying Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance?
IUL may be more suitable for individuals who want additional wealth growth opportunities, business owners seeking estate planning solutions, or individuals seeking retirement planning strategies with lifelong protection.
What should I consider before buying Indexed Universal Life insurance?
Indexed Universal Life (IUL) insurance offers lifetime coverage with policy value growth linked to the performance of a market index, subject to policy terms such as caps and charges. Before purchasing, it is important to understand how premiums are allocated, how charges and policy values work, and how index-linked crediting operates. You may also wish to consider whether this type of policy aligns with both your protection needs and long-term accumulation objectives, as policy value growth linked to an index can vary over time.

Choose among our four offerings:

















Plan A

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $50,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $2,000

Plan B

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $100,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $4,000

Plan C

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $200,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $6,000

Plan D

Sum Insured for accidental death or permanent disablement: $300,000
Medical expenses for accidents: $8,000

Important Notes
Here is are some key points to take note:

















All amounts shown are in Singapore Dollars
Benefits under this insurance will be payable only upon death, permanent disablement or injury due to an accident.
All those who are insured must be a Singapore citizen, Singapore Permanent Resident or foreigner who is holding a valid employment pass, work permit, dependent pass, student pass or social visit pass and residing in Singapore.
You or your spouse must be at least 18 years old and below the age of 70 at the commencement of this insurance. If enrolled under this insurance before 65 years old and there is no lapse in cover, this insurance is renewable until the age of 85. However, once you or your spouse is 75 years old and above, only  Plan A, B or C can be selected.
Your dependent child(ren) must be at least 1 month old at the commencement of this insurance and you can continue to cover them under this insurance until they are 21 or 25 (if the child is studying full-time in a tertiary institution).
If you or your spouse is a homemaker, full-time student, retiree or unemployed, only Plan A, B or C can be selected
If you, your spouse or your dependent child(ren) has/have more than one policy with us covering against Terrorism, the maximum amount payable for Acts of Terrorism for all the policies shall not exceed $500,000 per person.
When more than one person is insured under the policy, the total maximum amount payable under the policy is further subjected to a conveyance/event limit.
Premium rates are non-guaranteed and may be reviewed from time to time

You or we may cancel the policy by giving each other prior notice in writing. Please refer to the policy wordings for the cancellation conditions and applicable charges.
If you or your spouse wish(es) to nominate a beneficiary, please contact your intermediary for the relevant forms.

How much is my premium?
Your premium may vary, depending on:

















1
The type of plan selected



 





2
Your nature of work






3
Our underwriting requirements

Simply submit your claim form and any supporting documents to Broadspire at travel-uoi@broadspire.asia, or reach them at +65 6632 8639 within 31 days of your policy’s expiry.

Single Trip: If you cancel before your trip, you will get a refund minus S$25 (per insured person). No refund if the trip has already started. Annual Multi-Trip: If you cancel within 30 days before your policy starts, we’ll refund based on short-term rates, as long as no claims have been made.

You must be a Singapore Citizen, Permanent Resident, or hold a valid Employment Pass, Work Permit, Dependent Pass, or Long-Term Visit Pass in Singapore. Your trip must start and end in Singapore, and it cannot be for medical treatment purposes.

02/03/2026: Due to the escalating security situation in the Middle East, following strikes by the United States and Israel on Iran commencing on Saturday, 28 February 2026, under the terms of our policy, this situation falls under the General Exclusions relating to war and war-like operations, which apply to all sections of the policy. This means the policy does not cover any claim that arises directly or indirectly from war, hostilities, retaliatory strikes, or military actions.

As Singapore's #1 home insurer for customer experience¹, we provide comprehensive protection for your building, home contents, renovations as well as reimbursement for home assistance services, plus many other benefits. FWD Home insurance is also recommended as a complementary product for your Fire insurance (find out more here ).

We provide coverage if your pet dog or cat is stolen, passes away or is injured due to an insured event that happens at your home.

If you’re a landlord and your tenant defaults on rent, we cover up to S$3,000 per month.

plans.plan_overview:
With higher medical coverage for your helper, you'll be financially protected for medical expenses that may arise due to her illness or injury. In line with the Ministry of Manpower's (MOM) requirements, our hospitalisation and surgical expenses limits have increased to S$60,000 for all plans.

In line with MOM’s requirements, our hospitalisation and surgical expenses limits have increased to S$60,000 for all plans. You also have the option to increase the limit up to S$120,000.

We’re the only insurer who does not make you pay if your Security Bond gets forfeited by MOM.

plans.plan_description: Health Protection Protect you and your family against the financial burden of unforeseen injury and illness Our medical, personal accident and critical illness health insurance plans can help manage potential medical costs, so that you can focus on living your best life.
plans.plan_name: Protect you and your family against the financial burden of unforeseen injury and illness
plans.plan_overview:
Contact Us

Things to consider Protect yourself from unforeseen medical expenses with our health insurance plans so that you can focus on recovery. Hospitalisation benefits With rising healthcare costs, our suite of health insurance plans have you covered from diagnosis to recovery. Complement your medical coverage with critical illness protection While hospitalisation insurance reimburses your hospitalisation medical bills and certain outpatient expenses, critical illness insurance offers financial support, usually in a form of a lump sum payout, during your recovery. Available supplementary benefits Enhance your existing health insurance plans to enjoy more holistic coverage and peace of mind.

Finding the right health insurance plan Medical & Accident Critical Illness Be protected against unforeseen medical expenses, rising healthcare costs or unexpected injuries with our suite of tailored products and supplementary plans. PRU Shield and PRU Extra Comprehensive health insurance coverage to suit your budget and needs. PRU Personal Accident Personal accident insurance to cover minor mishaps or unexpected injuries even when you’re overseas. All Medical & Accident Plans Prepare for…

plans.plan_description: Life Protection Provide financial security for you and your family Our life protection insurance plans help keep your family financially secure in the event of the unexpected, such as disability or death.
plans.plan_name: Provide financial security for you and your family
plans.plan_overview:
Contact Us

Things to consider Find the most suitable life protection insurance plan so that you get ultimate peace of mind to achieve milestones. Meet your life insurance needs Get coverage for you and your family needs financially to ensure expenses and quality of life can be maintained should you become unable to work or meet with a premature death. Customisable plans to suit your changing needs Our life insurance protection plans provide flexibility for you to customise according to your changing needs as you progress through different life stages. Get protected at any life stage This includes protection for mum and baby during your journey to motherhood and being financially protected to overcome complications with our maternity insurance plans.

Finding the right life protection plan Whole & Term Life Maternity Mortgage Get comprehensive essential coverage no matter the life stage you’re at.
